company: PokerStars VR / Poker VR / PokerVerse VR
evidence: Multiple VR poker apps for Oculus Quest (Poker VR since 2019, PokerStars VR's Vegas Infinite, PokerVerse VR) let friends play multiplayer poker remotely, matching this idea, though not built by the MFM hosts.
Birthday Alarm was an already-established early-2000s viral birthday-reminder web service being discussed retrospectively, not pitched as new in 2019.
Ringo was an early-2000s social network (predecessor discussed alongside Friendster/Tribe.net) that already existed long before this 2019 episode.
Bebo was a full-featured social network launched in 2005 with photos and profile widgets, already historical by the 2019 discussion.
The Battery is a real, already-operating private members' club in San Francisco (founded 2013) being referenced, not a new pitch.
Brian Scudamore founded The Rubbish Boys junk-removal business in Vancouver in 1989, decades before this 2019 podcast recounted the story.
Scudamore rebranded Rubbish Boys to 1-800-GOT-JUNK? in 1998/1999 for national expansion, well before this 2019 episode.
The franchise model for junk removal across North America was already built out by 1-800-GOT-JUNK? in the 2000s, predating the 2019 discussion.
O2E Brands, Scudamore's home-service franchise portfolio (1-800-GOT-JUNK?, WOW 1 DAY PAINTING, Shack Shine), was an established multi-brand company by 2019.
WOW 1 DAY PAINTING launched its first franchise in Vancouver in late 2010, already an operating O2E Brands business by the 2019 episode.
